---
title: Node Classification
subtitle: classifying network nodes by type and connectivity
description: WFRC MasterNet node conflation — snapping abstract model nodes to UGRC/UDOT physical centerlines
author:
 - name: Pukar Bhandari
   email: pukar.bhandari@wfrc.utah.gov
   affiliation:
     - name: Wasatch Front Regional Council
       url: "https://wfrc.utah.gov/"
date: "2026-04-03"
---

## Setup Environment

### Import Libraries

```{python}
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

import gtfs_kit as gk

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd()))
from _src.node_utils import (
    nodes_on,
    count_links,
    assign_node_directions,
    extract_endpoints,
    assign_endpoint_directions,
    snap_nodes,
    snap_transit,
)
```

### Filepath Configuration

```{python}
config_filepath = {
    # Input files
    "in_centerlines":      Path("_data/raw/BangerterEdits.gdb.zip"),
    "in_network_links":    Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet - Link.gpkg"),
    "in_network_nodes":    Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet - Node.gpkg"),
    "in_gtfs":             Path("_data/raw/uta_gtfs.zip"),
    # Output files
    "out_node_classified": Path("_data/processed/nodes_classified.gpkg"),
    "out_node_snapped":    Path("_data/processed/nodes_snapped.gpkg"),
    "out_debug":           Path("_data/processed/debug_snapping.gpkg"),
}
```

```{python}
CRS_UTM = "EPSG:26912"   # NAD83 UTM Zone 12N — Utah (metric)
CRS_WGS = "EPSG:4326"    # WGS84 — required by Folium
```

---

## Load Data

### Roadway Centerlines

```{python}
county_list = ["49003", "49057", "49011", "49035", "49049"]
target_fips = "(" + ", ".join([f"'{f}'" for f in county_list]) + ")"

gdf_centerlines = gpd.read_file(
    f"/vsizip/{config_filepath['in_centerlines']}",
    layer="RoadsBangerterEtc",
    where=f"COUNTY_L IN {target_fips} OR COUNTY_R IN {target_fips}",
).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

# Strip leading/trailing whitespace from string columns (source data artefact)
for col in ["DOT_FCLASS", "CARTOCODE", "STATUS", "ONEWAY", "VERT_LEVEL", "POSTTYPE"]:
    if col in gdf_centerlines.columns:
        gdf_centerlines[col] = gdf_centerlines[col].str.strip()

print(f"Loaded {len(gdf_centerlines):,} features | CRS: {gdf_centerlines.crs}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

gdf_centerlines.head(1000).explore()
```

### Network Links

Only links present in the 2027 network (LN_2027 > 0) are loaded.

> **Note — network year benchmark:** Using `LN_2027 > 0` loads the future network
> state, which contains more built freeway and interchange links than the 2023 network.
> This is intentional for 2027-scenario classification. To benchmark snapping precision
> against the stable `main` branch results, temporarily revert to `LN_2023 > 0` and
> compare node counts — the ~150 node increase is expected from the denser 2027 network,
> not a logic flaw.

```{python}
gdf_links = gpd.read_file(config_filepath["in_network_links"], where="LN_2027 > 0").to_crs(CRS_UTM)

print(f"Loaded {len(gdf_links):,} links (LN_2027 > 0) | CRS: {gdf_links.crs}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

gdf_links.head(1000).explore()
```

### Network Nodes

```{python}
gdf_nodes = gpd.read_file(config_filepath["in_network_nodes"]).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

print(f"Loaded {len(gdf_nodes):,} features | CRS: {gdf_nodes.crs}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

gdf_nodes.head(1000).explore()
```

### UTA GTFS Feed

```{python}
feed = gk.read_feed(config_filepath["in_gtfs"], dist_units="km")

gdf_stops  = gk.get_stops(feed,  as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_shapes = gk.get_shapes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_routes = gk.get_routes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

print(f"Stops: {len(gdf_stops):,} | Shapes: {len(gdf_shapes):,} | Routes: {len(gdf_routes):,}")
```

### Rail Stops

Filter GTFS stops to Light Rail (route_type 0) and Commuter Rail (route_type 2) only,
walking the routes → trips → stop_times → stops chain.

```{python}
# GTFS route_type: 0 = Light Rail (TRAX), 2 = Commuter Rail (FrontRunner), 3 = Bus
RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES = [0, 2]

rail_route_ids = feed.routes.loc[feed.routes["route_type"].isin(RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES), "route_id"]
rail_trip_ids = feed.trips.loc[feed.trips["route_id"].isin(rail_route_ids), "trip_id"]
rail_stop_ids = feed.stop_times.loc[
    feed.stop_times["trip_id"].isin(rail_trip_ids), "stop_id"
].unique()

gdf_rail_stops = gdf_stops[gdf_stops["stop_id"].isin(rail_stop_ids)].copy()
print(f"Rail stops: {len(gdf_rail_stops):,} of {len(gdf_stops):,} total stops")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

gdf_rail_stops.explore()
```

---

## Node Classification

### Functional Type

Each flag column is assigned using `nodes_on()` with a pandas query string, or plain
pandas expressions for N-range conditions. All FT codes reference the 2027 network year.

```{python}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Freeway FT codes (2027):
#   20–27, 30–36 = freeway mainline family
#   Exclude: 21, 31 (CD), 37, 38 (HOV), 39 (ManagedAccess)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
FW = list((set(range(20, 28)) | set(range(30, 37))) - {21, 31})

gdf_nodes["Freeway"]          = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, f"FT_2027 in {FW}")
gdf_nodes["CD"]               = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2027 in [21, 31]")
gdf_nodes["HOV"]              = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2027 in [37, 38]")
gdf_nodes["ManagedAccess"]    = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2027 == 39")
gdf_nodes["Expressway"]       = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2027 in [12, 13, 14, 15]")
gdf_nodes["Arterial"]         = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2027 in [2, 3]")
gdf_nodes["Collector"]        = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2027 in [4, 5]")
gdf_nodes["Local"]            = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2027 in [6, 7]")
gdf_nodes["Ramp"]             = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2027 in [28, 29, 41, 42]")
gdf_nodes["CentroidConnector"]= nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2027 == 1")
gdf_nodes["CentroidNode"]     = gdf_nodes["N"] <= 3_600        # TAZ N: 1–3600
gdf_nodes["ExternalNode"]     = gdf_nodes["N"].between(3_601, 9_999)
gdf_nodes["ConnectorOnly"]    = (
    gdf_nodes["CentroidConnector"]
    & ~gdf_nodes["CentroidNode"]
    & ~gdf_nodes["ExternalNode"]
)
gdf_nodes["FixedTransit"] = (
    nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2027 in [70, 80]")
    | gdf_nodes["N"].between(10_000, 19_999)
    | gdf_nodes["N"].between(50_000, 59_999)
)
```

```{python}
flag_cols = [
    "N", "Freeway", "CD", "Expressway", "Arterial", "Collector", "Local",
    "Ramp", "HOV", "FixedTransit", "CentroidConnector", "CentroidNode",
    "ExternalNode", "ConnectorOnly",
]
gdf_nodes[flag_cols].head(20)
```

#### Summary

```{python}
gdf_nodes[flag_cols[1:]].sum().rename("NodeCount").to_frame()
```

### Link Count and Direction Assignment

Pseudonodes (LinkCount == 2) are shape vertices — they have no physical counterpart
endpoint and are excluded from all snapping passes.

`assign_node_directions` produces two direction columns:

- `link_directions` — freeway-priority cascade: if a node has any freeway links, only
  those links' DIRECTION values are pooled. Interchange link directions are used only
  when no freeway links are present.
- `fw_directions` — freeway links only. Used in gore pass (b) so that the directional
  signal comes exclusively from the freeway side of a mixed node. For a gore node with
  multiple freeway links in opposing directions, the majority-vote direction wins.

```{python}
gdf_nodes = count_links(gdf_nodes, gdf_links)
gdf_nodes = assign_node_directions(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, freeway_ft_codes=FW)

gdf_nodes[["N", "LinkCount", "link_directions", "fw_directions"]].head(20)
```

#### Distribution of Link Counts

```{python}
link_count_freq = gdf_nodes["LinkCount"].value_counts().sort_index()
max_val = link_count_freq.max()

BASE_COLOR     = "#B4B2A9"
ACCENT_COLOR   = "#1D9E75"
PSEUDO_COLOR   = "#EF9F27"
LABEL_COLOR    = "#444441"
SUBTITLE_COLOR = "#888780"
GRID_COLOR     = "#D3D1C7"

ACCENT_COUNTS = {4, 6, 8}
PSEUDO_COUNTS = {2}


def bar_color(link_count):
    if link_count in PSEUDO_COUNTS:
        return PSEUDO_COLOR
    if link_count in ACCENT_COUNTS:
        return ACCENT_COLOR
    return BASE_COLOR


bar_colors = [bar_color(i) for i in link_count_freq.index]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5.5))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
ax.set_facecolor("white")

ax.yaxis.grid(True, color=GRID_COLOR, linewidth=0.6, zorder=0)
ax.set_axisbelow(True)

bars = ax.bar(
    link_count_freq.index, link_count_freq.values,
    color=bar_colors, width=0.75, zorder=2,
)

for bar in bars:
    h = bar.get_height()
    ax.text(
        bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
        h + max_val * 0.012,
        f"{int(h):,}",
        ha="center", va="bottom",
        fontsize=9.5, color=LABEL_COLOR,
    )

for spine in ["top", "right", "left"]:
    ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["bottom"].set_color(GRID_COLOR)

ax.set_xticks(link_count_freq.index)
ax.set_xticklabels(link_count_freq.index, fontsize=11, color=LABEL_COLOR)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{int(x):,}"))
ax.tick_params(axis="y", labelsize=9.5, colors=SUBTITLE_COLOR, length=0)
ax.tick_params(axis="x", length=0)
ax.set_ylim(0, max_val * 1.18)
ax.set_xlabel("Number of links connected to node", fontsize=10.5, color=SUBTITLE_COLOR, labelpad=10)

legend_elements = [
    plt.Rectangle((0, 0), 1, 1, color=ACCENT_COLOR),
    plt.Rectangle((0, 0), 1, 1, color=PSEUDO_COLOR),
    plt.Rectangle((0, 0), 1, 1, color=BASE_COLOR),
]
ax.legend(
    legend_elements,
    ["Typical intersection (4, 6, 8)", "Pseudonode (2)", "Other"],
    loc="upper right", fontsize=9, frameon=False,
    labelcolor=SUBTITLE_COLOR, handlelength=1, handleheight=0.8,
)

fig.text(0.13, 1.01, "Most nodes connect to 4, 6, or 8 links",
         fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", color=LABEL_COLOR, transform=ax.transAxes)
fig.text(0.13, 0.94, f"Distribution of {len(gdf_nodes):,} network nodes by link count",
         fontsize=10.5, color=SUBTITLE_COLOR, transform=ax.transAxes)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Export Classified Nodes

```{python}
out_path = config_filepath["out_node_classified"]
out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_nodes.to_file(out_path, driver="GPKG")
print(f"Classified nodes saved to: {out_path}")
```

---

## Node Snapping

All spatial operations are in UTM (metres). The approach is piecemeal — validate
each pass before proceeding to the next.

### Prepare Centerlines

```{python}
import folium
from shapely.geometry import LineString, Point
from shapely.strtree import STRtree

# Exclude retired and planned segments — only active physical geometry
active_cl = gdf_centerlines[~gdf_centerlines["STATUS"].isin(["Retired", "Planned"])].copy()
print(f"Active centerlines: {len(active_cl):,} of {len(gdf_centerlines):,}")
```

---

## Step E — Build Centerline Endpoint Layer

The endpoint layer is the foundation of the new snapping approach. Instead of
snapping directly to centerline segments (and extracting endpoints inside the
algorithm), we pre-classify every unique endpoint coordinate by what types of
segments meet there. This makes topology and direction assignments explicit and
inspectable in QGIS before any snapping happens.

### Step E1 — Classify Centerline Segments

Assign `is_freeway`, `is_interchange`, `is_surface` boolean flags to each active
centerline segment. These are used by `extract_endpoints` to tag each raw endpoint
record with the type of its source segment.

```{python}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Authoritative freeway identification by UDOT route number (DOT_RTNAME[:4]).
# This is more reliable than DOT_FCLASS which can be inconsistently coded.
#
#   0015: Interstate 15       0080: Interstate 80
#   0067: Legacy Parkway      0084: Interstate 84
#   0089: US-89               0092: Timpanogos Highway
#   0154: Bangerter Highway   0177: West Davis Corridor
#   0201: SR-201              0215: Interstate 215
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
FREEWAY_ROUTES = [
    "0015", "0067", "0080", "0084", "0089",
    "0092", "0154", "0177", "0201", "0215",
]

# is_freeway: mainline segments only — excludes ramps (R) and CDs (C)
# DOT_RTNAME[5] distinguishes mainline (space or other) from ramp (R) or CD (C)
active_cl["is_freeway"] = (
    active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].str[:4].isin(FREEWAY_ROUTES)
    & ~(active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].str[5] == "R")
    & ~(active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].str[5] == "C")
)

# is_interchange: ramp or CD segments — Ramp and CD are treated as a single
# interchange class throughout. A model node with FT 31 (CD) regularly sits
# at a physical ramp endpoint and vice versa — the distinction is unreliable
# in both network and centerline attributes.
#   LRS 6th char 'R' = official UDOT ramp
#   LRS 6th char 'C' = collector-distributor
#   POSTTYPE == 'RAMP' = local slip ramps without a full 11-char LRS name
active_cl["is_interchange"] = (
    (active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].str[5] == "R")
    | (active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].str[5] == "C")
    | (active_cl["POSTTYPE"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper() == "RAMP")
)

# is_surface: everything else — state highways, arterials, collectors, locals
active_cl["is_surface"] = ~active_cl["is_freeway"] & ~active_cl["is_interchange"]

print(f"Freeway segments:      {active_cl['is_freeway'].sum():,}")
print(f"Interchange segments:  {active_cl['is_interchange'].sum():,}")
print(f"Surface segments:      {active_cl['is_surface'].sum():,}")
```

### Step E2 — Extract Raw Endpoints

`extract_endpoints` pulls the start and end coordinate of every segment as a flat
point GeoDataFrame. Each row carries the source segment's type flags and key
attributes. No deduplication yet.

```{python}
gdf_ep_raw = extract_endpoints(active_cl)

print(f"Raw endpoint records: {len(gdf_ep_raw):,}")
print(f"  (≈2× the number of segments — one start, one end per segment)")
```

### Step E3 — Deduplicate by Coordinate

Group raw endpoints by rounded XY coordinate (millimetre precision). For each
unique location, aggregate the type flags from all contributing segments using OR —
a coordinate is tagged `is_freeway=True` if any freeway segment terminates there.

```{python}
# Aggregate topology flags per unique coordinate
ep_agg = (
    gdf_ep_raw
    .groupby(["x_round", "y_round"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        is_freeway    =("is_freeway",    "any"),
        is_interchange=("is_interchange","any"),
        is_surface    =("is_surface",    "any"),
    )
)

# Reconstruct geometry from rounded coordinates
ep_agg["geometry"] = gpd.points_from_xy(ep_agg["x_round"], ep_agg["y_round"])
gdf_ep_unique = gpd.GeoDataFrame(ep_agg, crs=CRS_UTM)

print(f"Unique endpoints: {len(gdf_ep_unique):,}")
print(f"  is_freeway only (pass a targets):     {(gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] & ~gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange']).sum():,}")
print(f"  is_freeway + is_interchange (gore):   {(gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] &  gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange']).sum():,}")
print(f"  is_interchange only (pass c targets): {(~gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] & gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange'] & ~gdf_ep_unique['is_surface']).sum():,}")
print(f"  is_surface:                           {gdf_ep_unique['is_surface'].sum():,}")
```

### Step E4 — Assign Endpoint Directions

`assign_endpoint_directions` calls `_resolve_direction` per unique endpoint,
aggregating direction from all contributing raw segments with freeway-first priority:

- Freeway segments present → FULLNAME token → LRS P/N fallback (freeway segments only)
- Interchange-only → DOT_RTNAME[4] P/N → "NB,SB" or "EB,WB" (vague but correct group)
- Surface or unresolved → empty string

```{python}
gdf_ep_unique = assign_endpoint_directions(gdf_ep_unique, gdf_ep_raw)

print(f"Endpoints with direction assigned: {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_allowed_dirs'] != '').sum():,}")
print(f"Endpoints direction-agnostic:      {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_allowed_dirs'] == '').sum():,}")
gdf_ep_unique[["x_round", "y_round", "is_freeway", "is_interchange", "is_surface", "ep_allowed_dirs"]].head(20)
```

### Step E5 — Export Endpoint Layer for QGIS Inspection

Inspect topology classification and direction assignments before running any snapping.
Open `debug_snapping.gpkg → endpoint_layer` in QGIS and verify that:

- Freeway-only endpoints are correctly identified (no ramp/CD bleeds)
- Gore endpoints sit at the physical junction between mainline and ramp geometry
- Direction strings match the expected cardinal for each location

```{python}
debug_path = config_filepath["out_debug"]
debug_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_ep_unique.to_file(debug_path, layer="endpoint_layer", driver="GPKG")
print(f"Endpoint layer exported to: {debug_path} → layer='endpoint_layer'")
```

---

## Step 1 — Freeway and Interchange Node Snapping

### Step 1a — Build Node Snap Masks

Two binary flags describe every node's topological role. Their combination
determines which endpoint pool and distance threshold apply.

```{python}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# no_pseudo: exclude shape vertices — LinkCount == 2 nodes are abstract
#            geometry handles with no physical endpoint counterpart.
#
# is_fwy_node: touches at least one freeway mainline link (FT in FW family).
#              ManagedAccess-only nodes excluded — they sit on HOT lanes
#              with no corresponding mainline endpoint.
#
# is_ic_node:  touches at least one ramp or CD link.
#
# Pass assignment:
#   is_fwy_node=T, is_ic_node=F  →  Pass (a): freeway-only endpoints
#   is_fwy_node=T, is_ic_node=T  →  Pass (b): gore endpoints
#   is_fwy_node=F, is_ic_node=T  →  Pass (c): interchange-only endpoints
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
no_pseudo   = gdf_nodes["LinkCount"] != 2
is_fwy_node = gdf_nodes["Freeway"] & ~gdf_nodes["ManagedAccess"] & no_pseudo
is_ic_node  = (gdf_nodes["Ramp"] | gdf_nodes["CD"]) & no_pseudo

print(f"Pass (a) — freeway-only:    {(is_fwy_node & ~is_ic_node).sum():,}")
print(f"Pass (b) — gore (mixed):    {(is_fwy_node &  is_ic_node).sum():,}")
print(f"Pass (c) — interchange-only:{(~is_fwy_node & is_ic_node).sum():,}")
print(f"ManagedAccess-only (skipped):"
      f"{(gdf_nodes['Freeway'] & gdf_nodes['ManagedAccess'] & ~is_ic_node & no_pseudo).sum():,}")
```

### Step 1b — Freeway Neighbor Direction Lookup (for Pass c)

For interchange-only nodes (pass c), direction on the node side comes from
freeway mainline links connected one hop away in the network graph. This lookup
is done explicitly here so the logic is visible and the result is inspectable.

We walk the link table: for each interchange-only node N, find all links where
A == N or B == N. If any of those links is a freeway link (FT in FW), pull its
DIRECTION value. If the neighbor is itself a freeway node, inherit its
`fw_directions`. The enriched direction is stored in a temporary column
`ic_fw_directions` used only in pass (c) snapping.

```{python}
# Build a flat edge list: (node_N, neighbor_N, FT, DIRECTION)
edges_a = gdf_links[["A", "B", "FT_2027", "DIRECTION"]].rename(columns={"A": "node", "B": "neighbor"})
edges_b = gdf_links[["B", "A", "FT_2027", "DIRECTION"]].rename(columns={"B": "node", "A": "neighbor"})
edges = pd.concat([edges_a, edges_b], ignore_index=True)
edges["is_freeway_link"] = edges["FT_2027"].isin(set(FW))

# For each interchange-only node, collect DIRECTION values from adjacent
# freeway links only.
CARDINAL = {"NB", "SB", "EB", "WB"}
ic_only_nodes = gdf_nodes.loc[~is_fwy_node & is_ic_node, "N"].values
ic_node_set = set(ic_only_nodes)

ic_fw_dir_map: dict[int, str] = {}
for node_n in ic_node_set:
    adj_fw = edges[(edges["node"] == node_n) & (edges["is_freeway_link"])]
    if adj_fw.empty:
        ic_fw_dir_map[node_n] = ""
        continue
    dirs = set()
    for d in adj_fw["DIRECTION"].dropna():
        d = str(d).strip().upper()
        if d in CARDINAL:
            dirs.add(d)
    ic_fw_dir_map[node_n] = ",".join(sorted(dirs))

# Store on the nodes GeoDataFrame as a temporary working column
gdf_nodes["ic_fw_directions"] = gdf_nodes["N"].map(ic_fw_dir_map).fillna("")

# Diagnostic: how many interchange-only nodes resolved a freeway direction?
resolved = (gdf_nodes.loc[~is_fwy_node & is_ic_node, "ic_fw_directions"] != "").sum()
total_ic = (~is_fwy_node & is_ic_node).sum()
print(f"Interchange-only nodes with freeway neighbor direction: {resolved:,} of {total_ic:,}")
print(f"  (remaining {total_ic - resolved:,} have no adjacent freeway link — direction-agnostic)")
```

### Step 1c — Pre-Snap Validation

Check nearest-endpoint distances before snapping to confirm coverage and thresholds.

```{python}
# Build endpoint pools used in each pass
ep_fwy_only = gdf_ep_unique[
     gdf_ep_unique["is_freeway"] & ~gdf_ep_unique["is_interchange"]
].copy()

ep_gore = gdf_ep_unique[
     gdf_ep_unique["is_freeway"] &  gdf_ep_unique["is_interchange"]
].copy()

ep_ic_only = gdf_ep_unique[
    ~gdf_ep_unique["is_freeway"] &  gdf_ep_unique["is_interchange"] & ~gdf_ep_unique["is_surface"]
].copy()
# NOTE on ep_ic_only: The `& ~is_surface` filter intentionally excludes endpoints
# where a ramp terminates onto a surface arterial (Ramp-to-Arterial nodes).
# Those endpoints also carry is_surface=True because the arterial segment
# contributes to the same coordinate via OR aggregation in Step E3.
# Result: Ramp-to-Arterial nodes fail Pass (c) with "exceeded_threshold" and
# are recycled into Step 2 (Surface snapping) — which is the correct physical
# target anyway.
# This is now safe because snap_nodes() allows "exceeded_threshold" nodes to
# retry in subsequent passes (the state-lockout bug is fixed in node_utils.py).

print(f"Pass (a) endpoint pool — freeway-only:    {len(ep_fwy_only):,}")
print(f"Pass (b) endpoint pool — gore:            {len(ep_gore):,}")
print(f"Pass (c) endpoint pool — interchange-only:{len(ep_ic_only):,}")
```

```{python}
# Distance distribution for all freeway-eligible nodes to their nearest endpoint
all_fwy_eligible = gdf_nodes[is_fwy_node | is_ic_node].copy()
all_ep_fwy = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["is_freeway"] | gdf_ep_unique["is_interchange"]]

tree_pre = STRtree(all_ep_fwy.to_crs(CRS_UTM).geometry.values)
dists = np.array([
    g.distance(all_ep_fwy.geometry.iloc[tree_pre.nearest(g)])
    for g in all_fwy_eligible.to_crs(CRS_UTM).geometry
])
all_fwy_eligible["nearest_ep_m"] = np.round(dists, 1)

print(f"\nNearest endpoint distance (m) — all freeway + interchange eligible nodes:")
print(f"  min:    {dists.min():.1f}")
print(f"  median: {np.median(dists):.1f}")
print(f"  p75:    {np.percentile(dists, 75):.1f}")
print(f"  p95:    {np.percentile(dists, 95):.1f}")
print(f"  max:    {dists.max():.1f}")
print(f"\n  ≤  50m: {(dists <=  50).sum():,}  ({(dists <=  50).mean()*100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  ≤ 150m: {(dists <= 150).sum():,}  ({(dists <= 150).mean()*100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  ≤ 300m: {(dists <= 300).sum():,}  ({(dists <= 300).mean()*100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  > 300m: {(dists >  300).sum():,}  ({(dists >  300).mean()*100:.0f}%)")
```

### Step 1d — Snap Freeway and Interchange Nodes

Three passes in topology-priority order. Each pass only attempts nodes with
`snap_rule == "none"` so earlier passes are never overwritten.

```{python}
gdf_nodes_snapped = gdf_nodes.copy()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pass (a) — Freeway-only nodes → freeway-only endpoints
# Direction: link_directions (all freeway links, since is_fwy_node & ~is_ic_node
#            guarantees no interchange links on these nodes)
# Threshold: 500m
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    ep_fwy_only,
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & ~is_ic_node,
    max_distance_m=500,
    label="Freeway_Only",
    direction_col="link_directions",
    target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pass (b) — Gore nodes → gore endpoints
# Direction: fw_directions (freeway links only — isolates the freeway-side
#            direction of a mixed node, ignoring ramp/CD link directions)
# Threshold: 250m
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    ep_gore,
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & is_ic_node,
    max_distance_m=250,
    label="Freeway_Gore",
    direction_col="fw_directions",
    target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pass (c) — Interchange-only nodes → interchange-only endpoints
# Direction: ic_fw_directions (direction inherited from adjacent freeway
#            mainline link, one hop in the network graph — Step 1b above)
# Threshold: 100m (tight — ramp merge/split points are physically close)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    ep_ic_only,
    node_mask=~is_fwy_node & is_ic_node,
    max_distance_m=100,
    label="Interchange_Only",
    direction_col="ic_fw_directions",
    target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
)
```

### Step 1e — Post-Snap Distance Summary

```{python}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Diagnostic: how many interchange-only nodes failed Pass (c) and will be
# recycled into Step 2 (Surface snapping)?
# These are Ramp-to-Arterial nodes whose physical endpoint touches a surface
# street — the ep_ic_only pool correctly excludes those coordinates, so the
# node fails here and picks up its true surface counterpart in Step 2.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ic_exceeded = gdf_nodes_snapped[
    (~is_fwy_node & is_ic_node)
    & (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold")
]
print(f"Interchange-only nodes recycling to Step 2 (Surface): {len(ic_exceeded):,}")
print(f"  (These are Ramp-to-Arterial nodes — expected, not a failure)")
```

```{python}
ALL_SNAP_RULES = ["Freeway_Only", "Freeway_Gore", "Interchange_Only"]

for rule in ALL_SNAP_RULES:
    dists = gdf_nodes_snapped.loc[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == rule, "snap_distance_m"]
    if len(dists) == 0:
        print(f"{rule}: 0 nodes snapped")
        continue
    print(f"{rule} ({len(dists):,} nodes):")
    print(f"  median: {dists.median():.1f}m  |  p90: {dists.quantile(0.9):.1f}m  |  max: {dists.max():.1f}m")
    print(f"  < 50m:  {(dists < 50).sum():,}  ({(dists < 50).mean()*100:.0f}%)")
    print()

all_eligible = is_fwy_node | is_ic_node
exceeded = gdf_nodes_snapped[all_eligible & (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold")]
print(f"Nodes exceeded threshold (all passes): {len(exceeded):,}")
```

### Step 1f — Post-Snap Validation Map

```{python}
#| eval: false

fwy_snapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"].isin(ALL_SNAP_RULES)]
fwy_original = gdf_nodes.loc[fwy_snapped.index]

fwy_snapped_wgs  = fwy_snapped.to_crs(CRS_WGS)
fwy_original_wgs = fwy_original.to_crs(CRS_WGS)

connectors = []
for idx in fwy_snapped.index:
    orig = fwy_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]
    snap = fwy_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]
    if orig.distance(snap) > 0:
        connectors.append({
            "geometry": LineString([orig, snap]),
            "N": int(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "N"]),
            "dist_m": float(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"]),
        })
gdf_fwy_connectors = gpd.GeoDataFrame(connectors, crs=CRS_WGS)

center = [fwy_original_wgs.geometry.y.mean(), fwy_original_wgs.geometry.x.mean()]
m_post_fwy = folium.Map(location=center, zoom_start=11, tiles="CartoDB positron")

conn_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Displacement lines", show=True)
for _, row in gdf_fwy_connectors.iterrows():
    folium.PolyLine(
        [(c[1], c[0]) for c in row.geometry.coords],
        color="#888780", weight=1, opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"N: {row['N']} | moved {row['dist_m']}m",
    ).add_to(conn_layer)
conn_layer.add_to(m_post_fwy)

orig_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Original positions", show=True)
for idx, row in fwy_original_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x], radius=4,
        color="#E24B4A", fill=True, fill_opacity=0.8,
        tooltip=f"N: {gdf_nodes.loc[idx, 'N']} (original)",
    ).add_to(orig_layer)
orig_layer.add_to(m_post_fwy)

RULE_COLORS = {
    "Freeway_Only":       "#1D9E75",
    "Freeway_Gore":       "#534AB7",
    "Interchange_Only":   "#EF9F27",
}
snap_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Snapped positions", show=True)
for idx, row in fwy_snapped_wgs.iterrows():
    rule = fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_rule"]
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x], radius=4,
        color=RULE_COLORS.get(rule, "#888780"),
        fill=True, fill_opacity=0.8,
        tooltip=f"N: {int(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, 'N'])} | {rule} | {fwy_snapped.loc[idx, 'snap_distance_m']}m",
    ).add_to(snap_layer)
snap_layer.add_to(m_post_fwy)

folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False).add_to(m_post_fwy)
m_post_fwy
```

### Step 1g — Export Debug GeoPackage

```{python}
all_eligible = is_fwy_node | is_ic_node

# Snapped nodes across all three passes
fwy_snapped  = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"].isin(ALL_SNAP_RULES)].copy()
fwy_original = gdf_nodes.loc[fwy_snapped.index].copy()

# Displacement lines (original → snapped)
lines = []
for idx in fwy_snapped.index:
    orig_geom = fwy_original.loc[idx, "geometry"]
    snap_geom = fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "geometry"]
    if orig_geom.distance(snap_geom) > 0:
        lines.append({
            "N":               fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "N"],
            "snap_rule":       fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_rule"],
            "snap_distance_m": fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"],
            "geometry":        LineString([orig_geom, snap_geom]),
        })
gdf_fwy_lines = (
    gpd.GeoDataFrame(lines, crs=CRS_UTM) if lines
    else gpd.GeoDataFrame(columns=["N", "snap_rule", "snap_distance_m", "geometry"], crs=CRS_UTM)
)

# Unsnapped eligible nodes — exceeded threshold in all three passes
fwy_unsnapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[
    all_eligible & (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold")
].copy()

# Write all layers to the shared debug GeoPackage
fwy_snapped.to_file(debug_path,  layer="snapped_positions",   driver="GPKG")
fwy_original.to_file(debug_path, layer="original_positions",  driver="GPKG")
if not gdf_fwy_lines.empty:
    gdf_fwy_lines.to_file(debug_path, layer="displacement_lines", driver="GPKG")
if not fwy_unsnapped.empty:
    fwy_unsnapped.to_file(debug_path, layer="unsnapped_nodes",    driver="GPKG")
# Endpoint layer already exported in Step E5 — no need to re-export here

print(f"Debug package saved to: {debug_path}")
print(f"  snapped_positions:  {len(fwy_snapped):,}")
print(f"    Freeway_Only:     {(fwy_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'Freeway_Only').sum():,}")
print(f"    Freeway_Gore:     {(fwy_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'Freeway_Gore').sum():,}")
print(f"    Interchange_Only: {(fwy_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'Interchange_Only').sum():,}")
print(f"  unsnapped_nodes:    {len(fwy_unsnapped):,}")
```

---

## Step 2 — Surface Street Node Snapping

Arterial, Collector, and Local nodes snapped to surface street endpoints.
No direction filtering — snap to nearest within threshold.

### Step 2a — Build Surface Endpoint Pool

```{python}
ep_surface = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["is_surface"] & ~gdf_ep_unique["is_freeway"]].copy()
print(f"Surface endpoint pool: {len(ep_surface):,}")
```

### Step 2b — Snap Surface Nodes

```{python}
surface_snap_mask = (
    (gdf_nodes_snapped["Arterial"] | gdf_nodes_snapped["Collector"] | gdf_nodes_snapped["Local"])
    & (gdf_nodes_snapped["LinkCount"] != 2)
)
print(f"Surface nodes eligible: {surface_snap_mask.sum():,}")

gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    ep_surface,
    node_mask=surface_snap_mask,
    max_distance_m=250,
    label="Surface",
    direction_col="link_directions",
    target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
)
```

### Step 2c — Post-Snap Summary (Surface)

```{python}
surf_snapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "Surface"]["snap_distance_m"]
print(f"Surface nodes snapped:   {len(surf_snapped):,}")
print(f"  median move:           {surf_snapped.median():.1f}m")
print(f"  max move:              {surf_snapped.max():.1f}m")
print(f"  moved < 50m:           {(surf_snapped < 50).sum():,}  ({(surf_snapped < 50).mean()*100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  moved 50–200m:         {((surf_snapped >= 50) & (surf_snapped <= 200)).sum():,}")
```

### Step 2d — Surface Post-Snap Map

```{python}
#| eval: false

surf_snapped_gdf  = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "Surface"]
surf_original_gdf = gdf_nodes.loc[surf_snapped_gdf.index]

surf_snapped_wgs  = surf_snapped_gdf.to_crs(CRS_WGS)
surf_original_wgs = surf_original_gdf.to_crs(CRS_WGS)

connectors_surf = []
for idx in surf_snapped_gdf.index:
    orig = surf_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]
    snap = surf_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]
    if orig.distance(snap) > 0:
        connectors_surf.append({
            "geometry": LineString([orig, snap]),
            "N": int(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, "N"]),
            "dist_m": float(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"]),
        })
gdf_surf_connectors = gpd.GeoDataFrame(connectors_surf, crs=CRS_WGS)

center = [surf_original_wgs.geometry.y.mean(), surf_original_wgs.geometry.x.mean()]
m_post_surf = folium.Map(location=center, zoom_start=11, tiles="CartoDB positron")

for _, row in gdf_surf_connectors.iterrows():
    folium.PolyLine(
        [(c[1], c[0]) for c in row.geometry.coords],
        color="#888780", weight=1, opacity=0.5,
        tooltip=f"N: {row['N']} | {row['dist_m']}m",
    ).add_to(m_post_surf)

for idx, row in surf_original_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x], radius=3,
        color="#E24B4A", fill=True, fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"N: {gdf_nodes.loc[idx, 'N']} (original)",
    ).add_to(m_post_surf)

for idx, row in surf_snapped_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x], radius=3,
        color="#1D9E75", fill=True, fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"N: {int(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, 'N'])} | {surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, 'snap_distance_m']}m",
    ).add_to(m_post_surf)

folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False).add_to(m_post_surf)
m_post_surf
```

---

## Step 3 — Transit Node Snapping

FixedTransit nodes snapped to Light Rail (TRAX) and Commuter Rail (FrontRunner)
stops only — bus stops are excluded. Twin platforms merged to station centroid.

### Step 3a — Snap Transit Nodes

```{python}
rail_stops_grouped = []
for name, group in gdf_rail_stops.groupby("stop_name"):
    merged_geom = group.geometry.union_all().centroid
    merged_ids  = ",".join(group["stop_id"].astype(str).unique())
    rail_stops_grouped.append({"stop_name": name, "stop_id": merged_ids, "geometry": merged_geom})

gdf_rail_stops_grouped = gpd.GeoDataFrame(rail_stops_grouped, crs=CRS_UTM)

gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_transit(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    gdf_rail_stops_grouped,
    node_mask=gdf_nodes_snapped["FixedTransit"],
    max_distance_m=200,
    label="FixedTransit_Rail",
    target_id_cols=["stop_name", "stop_id"],
)
```

### Step 3b — Post-Snap Summary (Transit)

```{python}
transit_snapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[
    gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "FixedTransit_Rail"
]["snap_distance_m"]
print(f"Transit nodes snapped:   {len(transit_snapped):,}")
print(f"  median move:           {transit_snapped.median():.1f}m")
print(f"  max move:              {transit_snapped.max():.1f}m")
```

---

## Overall Snap Summary

```{python}
print("Snap results by rule:")
print(gdf_nodes_snapped.value_counts("snap_rule").rename("NodeCount").to_frame().to_string())

print(f"\nTotal nodes:        {len(gdf_nodes_snapped):,}")
print(f"Snapped:            {gdf_nodes_snapped['snapped'].sum():,}"
      f"  ({gdf_nodes_snapped['snapped'].mean()*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"Exceeded threshold: {(gdf_nodes_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'exceeded_threshold').sum():,}")
print(f"Not attempted:      {(gdf_nodes_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'none').sum():,}")
```

```{python}
bool_cols = [c for c in gdf_nodes_snapped.columns if gdf_nodes_snapped[c].dtype == bool]
gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold"][
    ["N", "snap_distance_m"] + bool_cols
].sort_values("snap_distance_m", ascending=False)
```

### Conflation Performance Metrics

```{python}
snapped_nodes = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snapped"]]

kpi_table = (
    snapped_nodes.groupby("snap_rule")["snap_distance_m"]
    .agg(
        Nodes_Snapped="count",
        Median_Dist_m="median",
        P90_Dist_m=lambda x: np.percentile(x, 90),
        Max_Dist_m="max",
    )
    .round(1)
)

print("=== SNAPPING PERFORMANCE KPIs ===")
print(kpi_table.to_string())
print(
    "\nTotal Nodes Exceeding Threshold:",
    (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold").sum(),
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
ax.set_facecolor("white")

color_map = {
    "Freeway_Only":       "#1D9E75",
    "Freeway_Gore":       "#534AB7",
    "Interchange_Only":   "#EF9F27",
    "Surface":            "#378ADD",
    "FixedTransit_Rail":  "#888780",
}

for rule, group_data in snapped_nodes.groupby("snap_rule"):
    distances = np.sort(group_data["snap_distance_m"].dropna())
    y = np.arange(1, len(distances) + 1) / len(distances)
    ax.step(
        distances, y, where="post",
        label=f"{rule} (n={len(distances):,})",
        color=color_map.get(rule, "#000000"),
        linewidth=2,
    )

ax.set_xlim(0, 300)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1.05)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
ax.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.6)
ax.set_title("Algorithm Precision: Cumulative Snapping Distances", fontsize=14, pad=15)
ax.set_xlabel("Displacement Distance (meters)", fontsize=11)
ax.set_ylabel("Percentage of Nodes Snapped", fontsize=11)
ax.legend(loc="lower right", fontsize=10, frameon=True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Export

```{python}
out_path = config_filepath["out_node_snapped"]
out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_nodes_snapped.to_file(out_path, driver="GPKG")
print(f"Snapped nodes saved to: {out_path}")
```
